user_query:
I need help syncing a cross-language next-sprint task summary for our team. Please work entirely in this workspace and use only the provided local files. Inputs to use:
- input/backlog.csv (canonical backlog records)
- input/team.json (assignee roles)
- src/js/app.js and src/java/App.java (inline TODOs)
- docs/README.md (update the marked section in place)

Requirements:
1) Inspect the directories src/js and src/java to extract TODOs that match exactly this pattern in comments:
   // TODO[id=ID,priority=N,due=YYYY-MM-DD,assignee=NAME]: message
   - Treat priority as an integer where 1 is highest.
   - Derive language from file extension (.js -> "js", .java -> "java").
   - Ignore any comments that don’t match the pattern above.

2) Load and parse input/backlog.csv with columns:
   id,title,component,language,priority,due,assignee,status
   - Status values such as "open" and "done" are possible.

3) Load input/team.json and enrich each task with assignee_role using the assignee’s role from team.json. If an assignee is not in team.json, set assignee_role to "Unknown".

4) Merge and normalize tasks by id (case-sensitive):
   - If a task id appears in both backlog and code, create a single unified record where title/component/language/priority/due/assignee/status come from the backlog, and include all code file paths where it was found.
   - If a task id appears only in code, create a record with status="open", component="" (empty), language derived from the file extension, title set to the TODO message, and include the code file path.
   - If a task id appears only in backlog, create a record using backlog fields and no code paths.
   - Always attach assignee_role from team.json if available.

5) Produce outputs/all_tasks.json containing an array of normalized task objects, sorted by id ascending, with these fields for each task:
   - id (string)
   - title (string)
   - language (string)
   - component (string; may be empty)
   - priority (number)
   - due (YYYY-MM-DD string)
   - assignee (string)
   - assignee_role (string)
   - status ("open" or other)
   - source (one of: "backlog", "code", "both")
   - code_paths (array of file paths where found in code; empty array if none)

6) Generate outputs/next_sprint.md with a table of the top 5 open tasks ranked strictly by:
   - priority ascending (1 is highest), then
   - due date ascending (earlier first), then
   - id ascending (lexicographic)
   Columns in this table must be: id, title, language, assignee, priority, due, source (use "both" if present in both backlog and code, otherwise "backlog" or "code"). Do not include tasks with status != "open" in this ranking.

7) Edit docs/README.md in-place: replace everything between the markers
   <!-- NEXT_SPRINT_START -->
   and
   <!-- NEXT_SPRINT_END -->
   with exactly the same table content generated in outputs/next_sprint.md (same columns and rows). Do not modify any other part of README.

Notes and constraints:
- Only consider files within src/js and src/java for TODO extraction.
- Do not add arbitrary tasks; only use what is in input/backlog.csv and the matching TODOs found in the code files.
- Ensure all outputs are deterministic given the provided inputs.
- Make sure both outputs/all_tasks.json and outputs/next_sprint.md are created, and docs/README.md is updated as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
